entry.title: Re: Credly certificate

Hi @Lillian_888 
Please share some more details e.g. the name and the URL of the BTM3 you are referring to. 
So I can analyze the case.
Kind regards 
Margit

entry.title: How to activate your S-User ID
entry.content:
Log in to SAP for Me and start exploring everything the platform has to offer today! 

                                     

A company that has a support contract with SAP and a Super Administrator set up within the company can create your S-User ID. You’ll receive an automatically generated welcome e-mail. This email will allow you to activate your S-User which can then be linked to your Universal ID.   

For whatever reason your S-User activation may have expired or perhaps you accidentally deleted the activation e-mail.  

Here are the steps to follow for an alternative activation method:

  1. Go to Forgot My Password.  

  1. Enter your S-user ID and click continue.   
  2. Check your inbox for an e-mail sent from SAP ID Service (ias@notifications.sap.com) to your S-user e-mail address.  
  3. Open the e-mail and click on the password reset link.  

 To note:   

  • The password reset link is valid for 2 hours from the time that it was generated from the system and can only be used once. If the password link expires, repeat the steps 1–4.   
  • You can only change your password once in a 24-hour period. 

  Now your S-User has been activated it will be available to add to your Universal ID! 

 Check out this blog to add your newly activated to your Universal ID: SAP Universal ID: Tips and Tricks - SAP Community    

For more information on S-User ID’s take a look at this blog: Learn All About S-User IDs! - SAP Community  

 Troubleshooting Tips:   

If you haven’t received the password reset e-mail after a few minutes:  

  • Check your SPAM/junk folder.  
  • Clear your browsing history, cookies, and cache and repeat the steps. 
  • Check that your company email settings are not blocking emails from SAP.  

 If you still haven't received the e-mail: Contact the SAP ID Service Team (SSO Team) at the following email address: sso@sap.com. You'll see more details on the SSO Team here: When and How to contact the SAP ID Service Team

                                                                                                                                                       

Thanks for reading!   

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have. 

If you have any questions, please ask a questions in the Q & A using All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community

Read more ##CustomerInteractionCenter blog-posts here!

entry.title: Re: Opening transaction in SAPGUI for HTML via URL fails...
entry.content:
I am now using a direct link to the WebGUI:

https://<host>/sap/bc/gui/sap/its/webgui?
~transaction=*QM02%20RIWO00-QMNUM=000200001234?sap-client=<client>

It looks like this approach is much more reliable than using the FIORI environment.

Do you think it's possible?

Regards

Regards

 Maurizio

entry.title: AW01N - values for ordinary depreciation have changed in depreciation area 02 and 32
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We went live on January 2025 for our India local company code that belongs to Group company located in USA. So typically we have set-up two FYV i.e. 0L- Jan to Dec and L1- Apr to March. But in S/4 2021 on prem in-order to use AA module your FYV periods should same further we have created additional ledger with R1 and assign same FYV (Jan to Dec) and done the required system set-up.

We followed year-end approach where-in the transfer date was 12.31.2024 (FAA_CMP) in which Highest FY is 2025 and Closed FY is 2024 for 0L and R1 ledger. Now, currently what we have observe after AA legacy data load of APC and Accumulated depreciation values through migration cockpit is whenever we are executing AW01N report system is showing message 'values for ordinary depreciation have changed in depreciation area 02 and 32' (India local-GAAP depreciation areas) SAP message AU390. Post which system is looking for AFAR not sure why? And not sure what went wrong and how to proceed further. Please note: Till now we haven't run any depreciation for the new company code

Best Regards,

Gourav Bose

entry.title: Re: Unable to finf RFC destination for QM connection
entry.content: Link is invalid.

entry.title: Two documents are saved instead of one
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I know that in principle BTE is not intended for validations, but in our system, there are validations located in BTE2218. Now we've noticed that in the following scenario, two documents are being saved, even though the user entered only one document. (Two records are inserted into BKPF). The scenario:

  • The user enters invoice data in FV60 and saves it (as parked).
  • Code within BTE2218 performs a check, and as a result:
    • A message is displayed (even if no message, the process is incorrect).
    • The process is broken by a leave screen.
  • The user corrects the data on the screen and saves it again (as parked).
  • The system saves two documents, not one. (Two records are inserted into BKPF).

From the debugging, it seems that with every save attempt, the system creates a new record (with a new document number). When the second save attempt is valid and a commit work is executed, both records are saved in the database, even though one record is invalid.

In the debug, it appears that the internal table for BKPF contains only one record each time. My assumption is that there is an internal table that gathers the two records, and in the end, the update to BKPF is done according to this table. I couldn't find this internal table or the place where the DB update is done .

How can this be fixed?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAC - Uploading Dimension with Append
entry.content:
Hi,

you can schedule it every day as per the loads observation and also check the Import settings.

entry.title: Re: Create Custom Mass Activity for your Program - Config
entry.content: Could you let us know how you created the CUSTOM parallelization object ? as it is checking for entry in table TFKDI

entry.title: Setup and usage of contract accounts
entry.content:
Hello,
We are reviewing our FI-CA administration and want to include more SD flows into FI-CA.
I was wondering if there are people in this community who can share their knowledge regarding the definition of contract account types and usage of contract accounts.
So for example: should we use one contract account type and create multiple contract accounts, or is it a best practice to define several contract account types.

We already have 2 divisions (electricity and gas) in use to support our IS-U processes. I expect some additional divisions to be created, because not all our products/services will fit into one of those divisions.

Best Regards,
Henk Hoogerbrug

entry.title: Re: SAP CDS View Entity - Joining Table View
entry.content: A little search gives the answer immediately: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/part-8-cds-views-joins-and-associations/ba-p/13406513

entry.title: Re: SAP dataflow parallel execution vs ABAP Dataflow for...
entry.content:
@avinash224  

To avoid running out of disk space, you should try to design the job in such a way that you don't have to save the data locally on the Job Server machine.

Second to avoid running out of RAM, you should also design the job in such a way that you don't have to load much of the data in memory.

Other ideas i would recommend looking into is to partition the source and target to allow parallel reads and loads to increase performance and possibly avoid the timeout issues.

Avoid doing a lot of transformations in your job.

Try increasing the RFC timeout to increase avoid RFC timeouts.

A good place to start is here Performance Optimization Guide

entry.title: Re: Positioning SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:

Our support team is available 24/5 and is happy to help!
Please make sure to select "Practice Systems" as the “Service Category” and kindly fill out the appropriate  form. When raising an inquiry, include detailed information in the description to help our team assist you better.

Please also check our Practice systems support site https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter/learninghub-subscription/practice-systems 
There you find a wealth of resources including how-to guides, troubleshooting tips, and detailed FAQs, all designed to help you quickly resolve any issues you may encounter on SAP Learning and improve your learner experience.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.content:
Dear all,

I am currently working on a seemingly easy SAC problem. My question is if it is posible to hide the SAC story load buffering screen, when opening a story:

So instead of seeing the above it should be blank.

Reason for that: I have created a landing page with a navigation and I would like to create the impression that everything is part of an integrated tool. is it possible to achieve this look and feel. If so, how could you achieve that? I have tried the Application.hideBusyIndicator(), but that did not do anything.

Thank you very much in advance!

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content: @StephanieMarley Thank you 🙂

entry.title: How to check backend & cloud connectivity for Data Provisioning Agent
entry.content:
How to check backend and cloud connection details for Data Provisioning Agent? 

Background: We're moving our DP agent from on prem to 3-rd party cloud provider, and we'd like to know the hosts that have to included in the Firewall configuration.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check backend & cloud connectivity for Data Pr...
entry.content:
• The DP agent itself is using 2 ports of its host - admin + listener ports.
    • These are visible in the dpagentconfig.ini file, or in the dp agent configuration tool.
      • dpagentconfig.ini: framework.listenerPort and framework.adminPort
      • Configuration tool: Option 3 (Agent Preferences) -> Option 1 (Display Agent Preferences).
  • The DP agent can connect to ONE target HANA system through a single host + port..
    • You can check this in the dpagenconfig.ini as well. Look for the hana.server and hana.port values.
  • The dp agent can connect to multiple remote systems (sources)
    • These connection details can be checked from web based dev workbench / HanaStudio / DB explorer, or any other DB management tool you're using on the target system. Open Provisioning -> Remote Sources, click into any remote source and check the remote source configuration window that opens. hostname, IP + port, and every other details will show up there

entry.title: Creation of accounts
entry.content:
Hello

Do we need to create g/l accounts , posting keys, customer & vendor accounts in SAP ?

entry.title: Are you an Enterprise Architect and looking for learning material on how to using SAP LeanIX?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Mastering the Application Rationalization with SAP LeanIX learning journey.

Overview
This Learning Journey is designed to guide you though the beginner workshop for Enterprise Architects. It helps you build fundamental skills in managing application landscapes and learn how to effectively scope, plan, and execute an application rationalization process using SAP LeanIX.

Learning objectives
Upon completing this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Understand key concepts of application landscape management.
  • Gain practical experience in running a rationalization assessment.

 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAP C4C V2 Extension Scenarios on Custom Fields
entry.content: Thank you so Much Molly... Have a wonderful day!!!

entry.title: How to mass create handling units of with mixed materials
entry.content:
First, create a packing instruction using the app “Create Packing Instruction – Master Data”.
For that, you need a business role that is, for example, based on the predelivered business role template: Master Data Specialist – Handling Unit Data (SAP_BR_HU_MASTER_SPECIALIST).

  1. Open the app “Create Packing Instruction – Master Data”, enter a name and press “Continue”.

     

  2. Under the tab “Components” provide the packaging material for item category “Packaging Materials (P)”.

     

  3. Next, enter the materials with the target quantities that you want to pack with this packing instruction, using item category “Material (M)”.
  4. Press “Save”.

Second, create a determination record using the app “Create Determination Record – Packing Instruction”.
For that, you need a business role that is, for example, based on the predelivered business role template: Master Data Specialist – Handling Unit Data (SAP_BR_HU_MASTER_SPECIALIST).

  1. Open the app “Create Determination Records – Master Data”, enter a Determination Type and press enter.

     

  2. Select a Key Combination and press “Choose”.

     

  3. Enter the materials with the packing instruction and press “Save”.

Third, use the ‘automatic packing’ function for the determination type selected earlier for the determination record.

  1. In this example, materials are entered for an outbound delivery.
    In the app “Create Outbound Delivery – without Order Reference” (VL01NO), click on “Pack”.

     

  2. Press “Automatic Packing”.

     

  3. In the tab “Pack Material” you can see the handling units created.

     

  4. In the tab “Total Content” you can confirm that they have the desired structure.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere hierarchy with directory error
entry.content:

Modeling a basic Hierarchy with Directory in SAP D... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: SAP CDS View Entity - Joining Table View
entry.content:
looks like normal view is not supported.

probably you can create a cds view as workaround

entry.title: Reduce TR import runtime
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

 

We are using CALM to deploy TR's in our S/4HANA landscape. Currently there are around 700 Features created and it is taking 6 to 7 days to complete the deployment in each system in our landscape.

 

Recently, we built the system for dress rehearsal 2 , which took 6 days to deploy all features and associated TR's. But we in worry how mange in Prod environment as we will not get that long downtime to just for import the TR's.

 

We would request your help to know if we have any option to deploy multiple features (ie. Import all) or any other way we can deploy TR's in mass from CALM.

 

Thank you in advance

 

Regards,

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Reduce TR import runtime
entry.content:
Hi,
SAP Cloud ALM's Change and Deployment Management now offers Deployment Scheduler to help: "orchestrate status-dependent deployments for SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP (version 7.40 or higher), SAP S/4HANA on-premise, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Simply select your desired features, and the corresponding transport set will be imported as "Import All" into the designated target tenant."

Information on the Deployment Scheduler can be found at https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/deployment-scheduling-in-sap-cloud-alm/ba-p/14023018.

Best regards,

SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Re: From Application Logging to Cloud Logging: Service Innovation Guide
entry.content:
Good evening @sreehari_vpillai @mohit_bansal3  - I have read your comments and seems like I have the same problem, where we are also sharing Cloud Logging Instance at the Central Shared Subaccount and based on the Ingestion UserID/Ingestion Pwd / Ingestion URL from its Service key I have created User Provided Service in the other Subaccount where we will deploy our CAP app. Then I have configured mta.yaml the same as in your example and however during deployment I get the same error: "No SAP Cloud Logging credentials found".

How have you resolved this?

entry.title: Problem moving GL account to QAS
entry.content:
We are trying to move an G/L account to QAS through a request and the account is not being created in QAS.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Problem moving GL account to QAS
entry.content:
Check the implementation status of account in App Manage G/L Account Master.

 

As referenced in the help.sap.com documentation https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/35e42fcad6e644c5b51f563da6dc190d.html?locale=en-US

 

 "Accounts with status N or I remain in your lowest system and are not available in your higher systems. In a two-system landscape, this means that those accounts marked N or I are only available in your quality system. In a three-system landscape, this means that those accounts marked N or I are only available in your development system.

Only those accounts that you set to status P are transported to your production system. "

Review documentation in full.

entry.title: Re: How to prevent new pricing performed when Item catego...
entry.content: Hello Vikas, this is not possible in public cloud. You cannot change the pricing change, and what it happening in your system is the standard behavior.

entry.title: Re: Let the Gamification Begin!
entry.content:
@Johan_Hakkesteegt @harrystrauss if you see questions with a correct answer that has not been accepted, please always feel free to send an email to community@sap.com. We can help. 

We are also working on the motivational aspect for those of you who answer questions, please stay tuned.

entry.title: Re: Payment Wizard didn't generate any documents
entry.content:
Hi @Tiago_Goncalves ,

There are some things that might result in a payment wizard with no payments processed. But first and foremost, check the "Non-Included Transactions" report after finishing the wizard. It may indicate which transactions could not be processed and why.


Check these items before running the payment wizard to avoid blocking payment creation:

  • House bank (G/L account).
  • BP Master Data (BP bank account, payment method, payment block).
  • Max Outgoing Amount (PW Step 5) not enough to cover the documents being paid.

Let the above and let us know the outcome.

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: How to download/extract custom Fiori app source code...

entry.title: GRAC_UPDATE_BR_ASSIGNMENT not working as expected
entry.content: 3331664 - BRM: Program to mass update of business role in update assignment was implement in our GRC system and is not working as expected.  When we make updates to Business Roles through NWBC and try to update users assigned to the roles using the new program no actions are taken.

entry.title: Date and others atribute association
entry.content:
Hello team,

I'm currently testing the SAP Datasphere trial and building a test model with the attributes "Year" (Date), "Organica," "FF," and "Economica." However, when I preview the data, the Year appears merged with the other attributes.

Each attribute has its own key, and the Year is set as the primary key. However, as shown in the screenshot, the information appears concatenated. Is there a way to separate the Year from the other attributes without using code for now? If code is required, what would be the best approach?

Thanks for your time!

Bernardo Santos

entry.title: Inquiry on Seamless Planning Activation and HANA Cloud Verification in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I would like to ask if the seamless planning functionality is currently active or if it is planned for a future release. I'm trying to utilize it, but I don't see the option to select the "Data Storage Location" when creating a new model in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). Additionally, I am unsure if my SAC system is running on HANA Cloud, which might be part of the issue. Could you advise how I can confirm whether my SAC system is running on HANA Cloud?

Please let me know if seamless planning is active for any tenants or if this feature will be released at a later date.

Thank you for your assistance!

entry.title: FM document after F-48
entry.content:
Greetings, experts.

 

Is it possible to generate a budget relevant FM document after a F-48? With the current configurations, the document generated is statistical. I'm not sure if it´s because of a derivation strategy or if it´s the default.

entry.title: SAC interview questions
entry.content:
For preparing SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). 

Questions:

1). What is SAC?

A cloud-based analytics tool by SAP for data analysis, visualization, and decision-making.

SAP analytics cloud is end-to-end cloud solution offered by SAP that integrates Business Intelligence , Planning, Augmented analytics, predictive analytics in a unified platform to make effective decision on organization's data. Basically, a SaaS that combines all analytical capability in a single product for all its users

2). What is Model in SAC?

 "Model" refers to the structured representation of data that is imported or connected to SAC for analysis, visualization, and reporting purposes.

It includes the data schema, dimensions, measures, and relationships necessary for analyzing and visualizing the data effectively within SAC.

 

3). Types of Model?

Live Data Connection :

model establishes a direct connection to an external data source, such as SAP HANA, SAP BW, or other databases. It allows SAC to query and analyze data in real-time without importing it into SAC.

Imported Data Model :

 type of model, data is imported from external sources into SAC and stored within the SAC environment. This allows for offline analysis and provides more flexibility in data manipulation and modeling within SAC.

4).What do you understand by digital boardroom in SAP Analytics Cloud?

A single presentation in the SAP analytics cloud combines multiple stories. hey give an overview of a business with summaries of different sections in one place.

5). How is smart prediction performed in SAP Analytics Cloud?

 Smart prediction is made with the help of SAP Machine Learning algorithms. The ML algorithms dig out the relationships between different data and build a formula known as a predictive scenario. We use this formula to predict future trends.'

Three predictive scenarios: regression, classification, and times series

6). What is Smart Predict

Smart Predict uses SAP machine learning algorithms to explore relationships in your dataset and build a formula – a Predictive Scenario – to predict future events or trends

7). What is  Smart Insights

With Smart Insights features, business users uncover critical hidden insights from data previously unseen.

 

 8). What is Smart Discovery

Smart Discovery gives an overview of your data by automatically building charts to begin discovering more about your data.

 

9). What is Search to insights

“Search to Insight” is one such feature that brings conversational AI to SAP Analytics Cloud wherein you can create a story by asking questions in natural language.

10). What are dimensions in SAC?

dimensions are attributes that provide context and structure to your data. They typically represent qualitative data, such as product names, customer

11). What are Measures in SAC?

Measures are numerical values representing quantitative data that can be analyzed, aggregated, and compared.

12). What are the types of filters in SAC?

In SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), there are several types of filters that users can apply to analyze data:

Story, page, widgets

—Dimension Filters: Allow users to filter data based on specific values or ranges within dimensions, such as product categories, regions, or time periods.

—Measure Filters :Enable users to filter data based on specific numerical values or ranges within measures, such as sales revenue, quantities sold, or profit margins.

—Time Filters: Specifically designed for filtering data based on time-related dimensions, such as dates, months, quarters, or years. Users can apply time filters to analyze data within specific time periods.

—Input Controls: Provide interactive filtering options, such as dropdown lists, checkboxes, or sliders, allowing users to select specific values or ranges dynamically.

—Page Filters:Allow users to create multiple pages within a story or report, each with its set of filters applied to analyze data from different perspectives.

—Rank Filters :Enable users to filter data based on ranked values, such as top or bottom performers within a dataset.

 

—Advanced Filters: Provide more complex filtering options, allowing users to create custom filter conditions using expressions or formulas.

These filters help users customize their data analysis and focus on relevant information based on their specific requirements.

 

 

 

# SAC

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Tenant migration made possible
entry.content:
So,

I can read between the lines that a manual migration is needed for moving from Datasphere to "Business Data cloud's Datasphere", is it so?

entry.title: Re: Use CRUD Opreations with SAP Datasphere Command-Line-Interface
entry.content:
Hey @miroslav_sedlar ,

Since this is not mentioned in the official SAP documentation (documentation), it is unfortunately not possible.

entry.title: Ways to identify revised PO
entry.content:
Hi, 

My biz team would like to know if there’s revisions made to a PO throughout the PO life cycle (until it’s closed), does SAP S4/Hana Public Cloud edition generate revision number or provide some sort of revision history to help identify when, who and what were the changes?

entry.title: Re: Is there a hard limit on the number bookmarks in SAP...
entry.content:
To the best of my knowledge, SAP Analytics Cloud doesn't impose a strict hard limit on the number of bookmarks in stories, but there are practical considerations to keep in mind.

While SAP doesn't explicitly state a maximum number of bookmarks per story in their documentation, users typically encounter performance issues when they create an excessive number of bookmarks (usually more than 50-100) in a single story. The exact threshold depends on:

1. The complexity of each bookmark (data displayed, visualizations, etc.)
2. Server resources and configuration
3. Overall story size and complexity

For better performance and management, SAP recommends:
- Using bookmarks strategically rather than creating too many
- Considering story hierarchies or multiple stories for complex reporting needs
- Regularly deleting unused bookmarks to maintain performance

entry.title: Re: What's new in FP03 of Transportation Management in S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2023
entry.content:
Thank you Eric! Great overview about our latest enhancements & innovations for 2023 FP03. 

Br, 

Sebastian Sav

entry.title: Defending Against AI Security Risks by Turning LLMs on LLMs
entry.content:
By Slim Trabelsi, Head of AI, Product Security, SAP Global Security and Cloud Compliance Product Security and Jay Thoden van Velzen, Technical Advisor, Office of the CSO

As a provider of both Business AI solutions and the AI Core platform that provides access to third parties and open source Large Language Models (LLMs), SAP is committed to Responsible AI. Part of that commitment is to continuously look for ways to protect against AI security risks.

SAP’s approach to only train with anonymized training data and use RAG and Business Connectors to systems with access control prevents most data leakage and privacy risks. However, there are other security risks that are not covered by that. We still must test the model behavior against the most common AI vulnerabilities:

  • Prompt injection, or coercing LLMs into unexpected behavior
  • Jail breaking, that is, bypassing safety measures built into the model
  • System prompt leaks where a model reveals its internal instructions

All LLMs are vulnerable to such attacks as they are to some degree. Guardrails and system prompts can be applied to mitigate against attacks, by validating input and output, as well as system prompts that instruct the LLM to restrict itself to approved responses. But to know what to defend against, you first need to know which attacks an LLM is vulnerable to. The models are updated constantly, and new attacks are identified continuously as well.

 

STARS

In 2023, a team in SAP’s Product Security organization started a project to see if testing for such AI vulnerabilities could be automated. SAP provides a wide variety of models through SAP AI Core, and the speed of development and proliferation of new models dictated that we had to scale up testing quickly. Specifically, the team wanted to see if LLMs could be leveraged to test LLMs for these vulnerabilities.

The initial proof of concept was promising, and the project developed into the Smart Threat AI Reporting Scanner, or STARS. This scanner tests all models that are provided on AI Core, as well as other models SAP evaluates internally, and is integrated in SAP’s secure development practices. It uses a GenAI agent to inject a variety of attacks into the LLM being tested to try to coerce it into bad behavior. To do that it uses two types of tool libraries to provide it with different attack types:

  • Static libraries with 1-step prompt injections
  • Dynamic, multi-step libraries

In the static attacks, the scanner runs through a series of common prompt injection attacks. A good example is the “ignore all previous instructions” attack, followed by an otherwise banned request, in the hope that the LLM would indeed respond to that and bypass instructions built into the model. The scanner plays each attack and records whether it succeeded or not.

The dynamic attacks take longer, and use LLMs in multi-step attacks, maintaining an ongoing conversation with the model under attack. The attack adapts to the response of the LLM and continues to coerce the model into bad behavior. It hooks into anywhere why the model gives a response that looks promising, and iterates to the point where the LLM misbehaves, similar to what happens in fuzzing. These tests, understandably, take for more time to run.

New attack libraries can be added as they become available, to add them to the body of test cases STARS runs through.

A Smart Judge

The results of the tests are passed on to a Judge to evaluate whether the attack was successful or not. The tool libraries reported some attacks as successful when actually they were not. The response might have been unexpected but was not considered harmful. To reduce false positives and confirm that an attack really succeeded in triggering a malicious response, the team developed their own Judge on top of an LLM. This Judge model is independent of the model that is being assessed, as well as the injection method (either static or dynamic).

Each LLM being tested is run through 10 tests in a row to evaluate its behavior. The response is not always the same, so the repeated tests gives a sense how often the model might misbehave under a particular attack. This ultimately results in a risk score for the LLM being tested that can be compared to others or to different versions of the same model. The results are displayed in a heat map showing which level and type of attack a model is vulnerable to. For instance, if the model is vulnerable to 1-step static attacks, it is likely to receive a high risk score.

The relative risks scores of LLMs are shared with developer teams to consider. They evaluate the security risk score along with ethics and safety tests, and suitability of the model for the developer team’s business use case. Considered together they can make an informed decision which LLM to use in their product. For a variety of reasons, SAP can’t disclose these security risk scores. However, STARS is now released as open source, so organizations can run their own model evaluations, whether against models available on SAP AI Core or otherwise.

Diagram showing STARS and its LLM model targets, as well as providing training data for LLM Defense

LLM Defense

It is good to get a sense of the relative security risks of using an LLM. It is even better to mitigate against them. A separate project aims to do that with LLM Defense, which is an inline solution that leverages GenAI to perform input and output sanitation on prompts and responses. Successful attacks in SMART are used to train LLM Defense to detect such attacks and defend against them. This takes three forms:

  1. Static patterns to detect attacks
  2. Pre-prompt reminder to the LLM to behave according to its system prompt
  3. Dynamic detection to identify tentative input and suspect output that diverges from the expected context

Static patterns protect primarily against 1-step static attack patterns. The pre-prompt reminder re-enforces the system prompt and instructs the model again “stick to that”. LLMs can sometimes apply only parts of their system prompt – especially when it is quite long – and such a pre-prompt reminder helps the LLM not to miss an important control. The dynamic detection looks for multi-step attacks that succeeded during SMART testing and blocks prompts or responses that look like that.

LLM Defense is deployed on BTP as an SAP internal-only self-service API.

 

Defenders’ Advantage

As others do, SAP believes that, while adversaries will use LLMs to their advantage, LLMs provides defenders on balance an advantage. Tools such as STARS and LLM Defense harnessing LLMs for defense help to stay ahead of adversaries trying to attack AI solutions. SAP is now automating STARS to immediately scan any new model that is added to SAP AI Core, and train LLM Defense before adversaries may be able to interact with the AI Business Solution.

We encourage you to try out STARS for yourself, and welcome contributions to the project.

 

More Information

STARS is released as open source. There are currently no plans to do the same for LLM Defense.

  • STARS Github repository
  • Secure, Empower, Advance – How AI Can Reverse the Defender’s Dilemma, Google, Feb 2024

entry.title: Re: What are the changes that we need to do in MIME while...
entry.content:
Hello Muheed,

Is hard to point an exact step by step guide, as this is unique to each Email Exchange Provider. Please check the following blog where we tried to described the steps to some of them.
https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blogs-by-sap/how-to-set-up-a-custom-mime-header-for-your-email-server/ba-p/13579042

Once you know your email exchange provider you can also ask to an AI tool  (e.g ChatGPT) for basic steps for a custom mime configuration on your email exchange provider. I am sure you will get great insights from it.

Best Regards
Gabriel

entry.title: Re: Deployment Scheduling in SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: Re: Deployment Scheduling in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi @shansari ,

The deployment scheduler is importing CTS-managed ABAP transport requests for the time being. In the next iteration we want to support imports of transports managed by SAP Cloud Transport Management. This includes BTP Workzone as well. 

Best regards,

Moritz

entry.title: Re: Release announcement of SAP Emarsys Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi Juan

Yes I believe so. I would recommend you raising a IT ticket and our support colleagues from learning should be able to help regarding this situation.

Regards

Svetha

entry.title: Release Announcement - SAP Emarsys Certification Update.
entry.content:
Hi everyone

We are pleased to inform you about our updated certification. These have a few additional and edited questions to match with our latest enhancements.

You can take this certification from :https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-emarsys-customer-engangement

You will need a S-User subscription to access this link.

In case you are certified already and want to retain your certification, please take our Stay Certified assessment. In this case you don't have to retake the certification. Please refer this blog for Stay Certified : https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blogs-by-sap/release-announcement-of-sap-emarsys-stay-certified/ba-p/13991326

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Blog series on my CPI + Camel learning journey
entry.content:
@EuricoBorges Thank you for your time and comment!

I think I managed to implement more or less stable karaf setup flow, so this week I plan to deliver next part of blog!

entry.title: Re: LP21 looks at "available Stock", not at "total stock" - this seems wrong! Wh
entry.content: Good afternoon! Did you find a solution to this error?

entry.title: Re: Mass changes in outbound deliveries
entry.content:
Hello, 

what exactly do you mean by mass change? In delivery there are not many fields that might be changed manually. You can either post goods issue, or do other activities that are available from Manage Outbound Deliveries App, where you have list of all your deliveries. 

Mass changes are mostly used for Master Data or Orders/ Purchase Orders.

I think this thread might be helpfull for you:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/how-do-you-mass-change-field-values-in-s-4hana-cloud/qaq-p/12325743

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Re: How to develop a custom outbound API in S/4 HANA Clou...
entry.content: Hello, please read: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/creating-custom-apis-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13563767

entry.title: Re: Success factors Compensation and Bonus configurations
entry.content:
Hi @Sanjukta1 

You can start with this blog: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/compensation-implementation-leading-practices-and-key-project-documents/ba-p/13469232

 

It's not an easy module, especially Variable Pay.

Best of luck with your learning,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Outbound SMS / WhatsApp Message from SAP Service Cloud
entry.content: Hi Kapil, have you had any luck figuring out how to call the Twilio APIs to send a message from SAP Service Cloud?

entry.title: Re: Bonus Calculation Explanation - Variable Pay
entry.content:
Hi @Olga_Skarla 

You can check out this blog if you are confused with Bonus Calculation formula. Everything is explained with visual examples. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/bonus-calculation-equations-in-variable-pay/ba-p/13536915

I would advise making all standard fields visible and relaunching worksheets and checking if you can find a standard field that calculates things as expected. If you don't then most likely something is up with your configuration and as per @catmoye you would need to show us more details about it (document everything with screenshot or a recording).

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Upgrade still running: Logon not possible
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We recently attempted to update our system, but during the process, the SUM got stuck. As a result, we decided to remove the SUM directory and restore our database from a backup file. Now, we are unable to log in to the system and receive the error message: "Upgrade still running: Logon not possible." We can only log in using the DDIC user.

I have added the SUM directory back and tried unlocking the system with the command ./SAPup unlockshd S69, but I encountered the error: "Fatal Error: Unknown unlock option 'S69'."

Has anyone encountered this issue before? Any advice on how to unlock the system?

S/4 Hana 1809 FPS01 
DB: Hana 2.0

 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Learning best practices for file namin...
entry.content: Kris, I agree with your naming convention! The 3 letter acronyms work very well and help to identify either a department, category or function. As you've suggested, LGL for legal, FIN for finance, SAF for safety, ONB for onboarding. LMS uses many entities together such as a class, content object, item, program, quiz so it's helpful when entities can be correlated or banded together. It makes it easier for everyone. I've also seen where each year a course is updated from the previous year. Rather than reversion the course, the course was copied and renamed the same name, but by year. Example, LGL 101 2024, LGL 101 2025 and so forth. When versioning is used, it generally starts with 1 and goes up in increments, version 1, version 2, version 3. It's always best to add comments, author, contact info as well.

entry.title: Has anyone successfully implementing Pay Ranges with Additional Attributes to Pay Ranges?
entry.content:
I'm investigating how feasible it is to add more attribute to the pay ranges for additional filtering beyond pay grade, geozone, legal entity. Has anyone done this successfully? If so, what steps did you take and how many additional attributes were you able to add?

TIA

Leslie

entry.title: From Tech Talk to Sweet Moments "A Memorable Evening in New York"
entry.content:
As February comes to an end, I had the pleasure of meeting two knowledgeable individuals, @Suresh_Sadhu  and @smarchesini . Our conversation centered around key topics in SAP, particularly sharing individual experiences of day-to-day work schedules and the latest updates from the SAP community meetup. It was an insightful exchange of ideas, and we left the conversation feeling inspired and more informed.

Beyond the work talk, we also shared some personal interests. It was interesting to hear about what drives each of us outside of the professional world, whether it’s exploring new hobbies, reading, or simply taking time to relax. These personal insights added a refreshing perspective to the conversation.

Afterward, we took a walk through the lively streets of Times Square, New York. The energy, bright lights, and constant activity made it a unique experience. It was a great way to unwind and reflect on the discussions we had.

To end the evening, we visited a familiar restaurant and bakery and enjoyed their famous cheesecake. It was the perfect treat to finish off a rewarding day, surrounded by good food and great company.

Overall, it was a fantastic evening filled with valuable conversations and moments of relaxation. I’m looking forward to more enriching experiences like this in the future!

entry.title: Re: Determining which tables are required to recreate dat...
entry.content:
Hello, 

yes - using F1 and then technical information is the best way to see what is the technical name of the field, that you are going to use for your purposes. 

 

Quite important for you, shall be information regarding "table category". If table category is transparent table, then it means that it is single-use table, that data might be easily accessed. If table category is structure, it means that there are plenty of other tables that together creates so called structure. It means those data depends on other data and tables in the system. 

If you are using SAP Public Cloud, you can also use View Browser APP and search for needed data. 

You can also check https://api.sap.com/ and search for APIs that you are willing to use, for material stock for example https://api.sap.com/api/API_MATERIAL_STOCK_SRV/overview

You can also use https://help.sap.com/docs/ and search for needed table fields.

If you are talking about transaction SE16N, then you might not use SAP Cloud, but ON Premise solution (so your tag is wrong) then you can read this: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/sap-standard-tables/ba-p/13241520 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/general-tables-useful-in-the-sap-s4hana-module-mm/ba-p/13974210

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/details-showing-important-tables-in-sap-fi-and-co-modules/ba-p/13205958

Hope that helps

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements Navigation to Object Page
entry.content:
Hi @sandeep_rs ,

How do we make sure the full context gets passed to the target appliction, to it navigates to the Object page?

The links you provided don't explain how to do that.

Thanks

Hi @LeslieLessig19 

As far as I know (and I'm thinking of one specific customer for who we had 6 attributes) this can be built in EC Comp and also in Comp module to align with EC.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰

The following is probably the best diagram of the holistic Business Processes that I have ever seen...

entry.title: MRP live and Transportation lane "All Products"
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

I use MRP live (with Product heuristics set in Advanced planning view) to calculate Purchase requisitions.

In ePPDS I created a Transportation lane from the Vendor to the Plant with only one line set as "All products".

I would like the MRP run to take into account this Transportation lane instead of Purchasing info record in S/4.

Do you know if this is possible and how to do it?

Thank you.
